input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to show them how to set up AppShell.xaml for a .NET MAUI app with:
1. A flyout menu with two sections:
   - "Dashboard" (single page)
   - "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
2. A Settings page accessible from the flyout
3. Route registration for a ProductDetailsPage (navigation target from the list)

I'll create a sample AppShell.xaml and the corresponding AppShell.xaml.cs with route registration.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Views"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
    Title="MyMauiApp">

    <!-- Dashboard: single page in its own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products: flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings: accessible from the flyout -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the setup:

**AppShell.xaml** – defines the flyout structure:

```xml
<FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" ...>        <!-- single page -->
<FlyoutItem Title="Products" ...>         <!-- two sub-tabs -->
    <Tab>
        <ShellContent Title="Active" ... />
        <ShellContent Title="Archived" ... />
    </Tab>
</FlyoutItem>
<FlyoutItem Title="Settings" ...>         <!-- single page -->
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs** – registers the detail route:

```csharp
Routing.RegisterRoute("productdetails", typeof(ProductDetailsPage));
```

**Navigating to the detail page** (e.g., from `ActiveProductsPage`):

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"productdetails?id={product.Id}");
```

Key points:
- Wrapping two `ShellContent` elements inside a `<Tab>` gives you bottom tabs within that flyout item.
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` is needed because `ProductDetailsPage` isn't part of the visual hierarchy—it's a push navigation target.
- Accept the query parameter with `[QueryProperty("Id", "id")]` on `ProductDetailsPage` (or use `IQueryAttributable`).
